description: Thailand's cultural capital in the misty northern mountains — ancient temple clusters within a moated old city, the world's best digital nomad infrastructure for the price, and an elephant sanctuary that changes lives.

neighborhoodRecs: Old City (Moat area) — Inside the moat, most temples, great guesthouses, walkable in the cool season.
Nimmanhaemin — Hip neighbourhood, coffee shops, co-working spaces, great restaurants, digital nomad hub.
Santitham — Local residential area north of old city, excellent cheap street food, authentic Chiang Mai life.
Chang Klan (Night Bazaar) — Tourist commercial area, good for shopping and street food, convenient to everything.
areasToAvoid: February–April: severe smoke season from agricultural burning makes air quality dangerous (AQI 200–400). Not advisable to visit.
Tuk-tuks without pre-agreed prices — set the price before you get in, ฿30–60 for most journeys within the city.
scamWarnings: Elephant sanctuary scams: many operators claim to be ethical but still use hooks and chains. Research thoroughly — Elephant Nature Park is the most reputable.
Temple entry scam: someone near a closed temple says it's locked but they know a 'secret side entrance' for a fee. Real temples are open during posted hours.
Fake monk collecting donations — real monks don't approach tourists for money. Give only to established temple collections.
Gem/handicraft export scam: 'government quality gems' offered for resale profit at home are never real and never profitable.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Songthaew (red shared taxi) from airport area — ฿20–30 per person to old city or Nimman, takes 20 min.
airportTransfer.fastest: Grab app (Uber equivalent) from airport — ฿80–120 to anywhere in the city, takes 15–20 min.
airportTransfer.recommended: Grab for solo or couple — convenient, air-conditioned, fixed price shown in advance.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Official taxi from CNX airport: ฿150 fixed rate to most central locations. Meter taxis also available at ฿35 flagfall.

tippingEtiquette: Tipping is appreciated in tourist contexts. ฿20–50 at restaurants, more for exceptional service.
Massage tip: ฿50–100 minimum, more for longer sessions at quality places.
Thai massage is a healing tradition — don't expect it to be a gentle experience. It's therapeutic and sometimes intense.
Dress modestly for temples — the Doi Suthep temple on the mountain has strict enforcement.
Wai greeting: press palms together at chest height and bow slightly. Don't initiate with service staff, let them lead.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Hostel dorm ฿150–300 | Guesthouse ฿300–600 | Boutique hotel ฿800–1800 | Resort ฿2500+
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Street khao soi ฿50–70 | Cheap restaurant ฿80–150 | Nice restaurant ฿200–400 | Cooking class ฿900–1200
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: Songthaew ฿20–30 | Grab ฿60–100 | Scooter rental ฿150–250/day | Bicycle rental ฿50–80/day
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Elephant Nature Park ฿2500 | Doi Suthep temple ฿30 | Thai cooking class ฿900–1200 | Muay Thai class ฿400

packingTips: Light layers for cool season evenings — mornings can be 15–17°C December–January in the mountains.
Good walking sandals — the old city moat area is walkable but uneven.
Mask for February–April — smoke season air quality requires N95 respirators, not cloth masks.
Scooter licence or international permit if planning to rent a motorbike in the hills.
premium.bookingStrategy: November–January is peak season with perfect weather. Avoid February–April completely (smoke). Book Elephant Nature Park 2–3 weeks ahead — it's the most popular activity in northern Thailand.
localTips: Khao soi (curry noodle soup) is Chiang Mai's signature dish — Khao Soi Islam on Charoenprathet Road is considered the best.
Saturday Night Market (Walking Street) on Wualai Road is more authentic than the Sunday Night Market. Arrive at 5pm.
The Doi Suthep temple drive at sunrise (hire a songthaew at 6am) beats the midday heat and crowds completely.
Nimman area's co-working café scene: CAMP at Maya Mall has 24-hour power outlets and free WiFi — popular with nomads.
Night Safari (government park) is largely disappointing. Skip it for the botanical gardens or evening temple visits.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: November to January: cool (15–25°C), dry, clear air, Loi Krathong lantern festival in November.
dayTrips: Doi Inthanon National Park — Thailand's highest peak, stunning waterfalls, hill tribe villages, 2 hours by car.
Mae Hong Son — Remote mountain town on the Myanmar border, 4-hour drive or short flight, untouched landscapes.
Lamphun — Ancient Hariphunchai kingdom capital 30 min south, beautiful temple (Wat Phra That Hariphunchai), almost no tourists.
visaInfo: Most Western nationalities (US, UK, EU, Australia, Canada) receive a visa exemption on arrival — 60 days for many passport holders as of 2024; check Thailand's MFA for your nationality
Thai Tourist Visa (TR) available in advance from Thai embassies — 60 days single entry; useful if planning a longer stay or multiple entries
The Chiang Mai immigration office (on the road toward the airport) handles 30-day extensions for 1,900 THB — a popular option for longer stays
Chiang Mai is a popular base for 'border runs' to Myanmar (Mae Sai) or Laos (Chiang Khong) to reset the visa exemption, though this practice is less guaranteed than it once was
METV (Multiple Entry Tourist Visa) allows 6 months of stays with 60 days per entry — useful for digital nomads spending significant time in Thailand
currencyTips: Currency: Thai Baht (฿ / THB). Chiang Mai is cheaper than Bangkok — street food from 40–80 THB, guesthouses from 300 THB/night
Best exchange: SuperRich and other exchange kiosks in the Night Bazaar area and near Nimman Road offer good rates; hotel exchange is poor
ATMs: 220–250 THB foreign card fee per transaction is standard across all Thai ATMs — use Kasikorn Bank for lowest foreign fees
Cash is essential for the Sunday Walking Street, Night Bazaar, and all temple entrance fees
Nimman area (the trendy neighbourhood) is increasingly card-friendly; the old city and markets remain predominantly cash
premium.simCard: AIS and True Move H have the best coverage in Chiang Mai city and across northern Thailand's mountains. Purchase at Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX) on arrival. A 30-day SIM with 30 GB costs 300–500 THB. Coverage in remote hill tribe areas and mountain passes can be patchy — download offline maps beforehand. eSIM via Airalo is a convenient alternative.
